title: Initialize storage on first use

story.i_want_to: have the app automatically create its storage directory and file on first use
story.so_that: I don't have to manually set up anything before using the todo app

rationale: A frictionless first-run experience is critical for a CLI tool. If the user has to manually create directories or files before using the app, they may give up or make mistakes. Auto-initialization ensures the app just works out of the box.

acceptance_criteria.description: When the app runs for the first time and ~/.todo/ does not exist, it creates the directory automatically.
When ~/.todo/ exists but tasks.json does not, the app creates tasks.json with an empty JSON array [].
When ~/.todo/tasks.json already exists, the app reads it without overwriting.
The storage initialization runs before any command is executed, so all commands can assume the file exists.
On first-run initialization (when ~/.todo/ is created), the app prints 'Initialized task storage at ~/.todo/tasks.json' to stderr so it does not interfere with piped stdout.
If tasks.json exists but contains invalid JSON, the app prints 'Error: Task file is corrupted. Please check ~/.todo/tasks.json' to stderr and exits with code 1.
If ~/.todo/ or tasks.json cannot be created or read due to permission errors, the app prints 'Error: Cannot access ~/.todo/. Check directory permissions.' to stderr and exits with code 1.
Writes to tasks.json use a write-then-rename pattern (write to a temporary file, then atomically rename) to prevent data corruption from partial writes or disk-full scenarios.

test_cases.name: Creates ~/.todo directory when missing
expected_results: Returns indication that this was a first-run initialization

test_cases.name: Creates tasks.json when directory exists but file is missing
Does not overwrite existing tasks.json
expected_results: File contents still contain the existing task

test_cases.name: Corrupted tasks.json shows user-friendly error
expected_results: Raises a handled error (not raw JSONDecodeError)

test_cases.name: Permission error shows user-friendly message
expected_results: Raises a handled error (not raw PermissionError)

test_cases.name: Atomic write prevents corruption on failure
preconditions: tmp_dir with existing tasks.json containing valid data
steps: Call storage.save() with data that triggers a write failure (e.g., mock disk full)

technical_notes: Use os.makedirs(path, exist_ok=True) for directory creation
Use os.path.expanduser('~') to resolve home directory
Accept optional base_dir parameter for testing (defaults to home dir)
Use tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile + os.replace() for atomic writes
Catch PermissionError and json.JSONDecodeError with user-friendly messages
Print initialization message to stderr (print(..., file=sys.stderr))
